breaking_changes.notes: Tauri 2.x has been incremental since 2.0 stable (Oct 2024). No breaking changes within 2.x minor/patch releases. Tauri 3.0 milestone exists (gtk3->gtk4-rs migration required) but is 0% complete as of 2026-03-03 with 13 open issues. No imminent risk to 2.x projects.

deprecations.notes: No deprecations announced for Tauri 2.x line.

details.description: iFrames bypass origin checks for Tauri API access control. Fixed in v2 betas before stable.
Improper scope validation in the 'open' endpoint of tauri-plugin-shell. Published 2025-04-02.

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Ensure tauri-plugin-shell is updated. Check github.com/tauri-apps/tauri/security/advisories regularly.

tauri.maintenance_details: Very active development. Regular releases (2.9.x Dec 2025, 2.10.x Feb-Mar 2026). Core team + community. CrabNebula backing.

vite.notes: Official Tauri docs verified with Vite 5.4.8. Vite 8.0.0 just released 2026-03-12 - Tauri docs not yet updated for Vite 8, but Tauri uses Vite's dev server via devUrl config, so Vite major version changes rarely break Tauri integration. Monitor for any issues.

react.notes: Tauri is frontend-framework agnostic. React 19 works fine via Vite.

roadmap_risks.tauri_3: Tauri 3.0 planning has begun (gtk4 migration). 0% complete. No timeline announced. Will require migration but likely 2027+. No risk to current project.
roadmap_risks.focus_areas: Team focused on mobile DX improvements and documentation for 2.x.

breaking_changes.notes: React 19.0 (Dec 2024) had breaking changes vs React 18: UMD builds removed, StrictMode double-invokes ref callbacks, TypeScript ref mutability changes, removed deprecated APIs (ClassicComponent, ReactChildren, etc.). React 19.1 and 19.2 had NO breaking changes - only improvements.

deprecations.notes: Legacy APIs removed in 19.0: ClassicComponentClass, ClassicComponent, ClassicElement, ComponentSpec, Mixin, ReactChildren, ReactHTML, ReactSVG, SFCFactory. These were moved to create-react-class. Not relevant for new projects.

details.description: Denial of Service and Source Code Exposure in React Server Components. Disclosed Dec 2025. Fixed in 19.0.4, 19.1.5, 19.2.4.

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: MUST use React 19.2.4 (not 19.2.1 or 19.2.3 which are incomplete fixes). Versions 19.0.3, 19.1.4, 19.2.3 have incomplete patches - update to .4 versions. Note: This CVE affects Server Components which Tauri desktop apps do not use, but update anyway.

react.maintenance_details: React team at Meta. Very active. 3 minor releases in 2025 (19.0, 19.1 Jun, 19.2 Oct). Regular patch releases for security.

reactflow.notes: @xyflow/react 12.9.0+ officially supports React 19. UI components updated Oct 2025.

shadcn.notes: shadcn/ui fully supports React 19. All components updated.

vite.notes: @vitejs/plugin-react v6 (for Vite 8) uses Oxc for React Refresh, dropped Babel dependency. Fully compatible.

roadmap_risks.react_compiler: React Compiler (formerly React Forget) continues development. Not a risk - additive optimization.
roadmap_risks.server_components: RSC not applicable for Tauri desktop. No impact.

version_gap.recommendation: Update to 12.10.1. Versions 12.9.0+ include React 19 + Tailwind v4 support for UI components. 12.10.x includes perf optimizations for zooming/panning.

breaking_changes.notes: No breaking changes from 12.8.2 to 12.10.1 - all within v12 minor releases. v12 breaking changes vs v11 included: renamed 'parentNode' to 'parentId', new package name @xyflow/react.

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: No known CVEs. Low attack surface (UI rendering library).

reactflow.maintenance_details: Active development by xyflow team. Regular releases throughout 2025-2026. Pro subscriptions fund development.

react_19.notes: UI components explicitly updated for React 19. Core library compatible earlier.

tailwind_v4.notes: Tailwind v4 CSS-first config requires importing @xyflow/react/dist/style.css in global.css after tailwindcss import instead of in App.tsx.

shadcn.notes: ReactFlow UI components use latest shadcn/ui patterns.

roadmap_risks.v13: No v13 announced. v12 is actively maintained.
roadmap_risks.migration_note: Style import path change for Tailwind v4 is the main integration change.

breaking_changes.notes: Tailwind v4.0 (Jan 2025) was a major rewrite with significant breaking changes from v3: JavaScript config (tailwind.config.js) replaced with CSS-first @theme directive, PostCSS plugin replaced with Vite-first approach using @tailwindcss/vite, new Lightning CSS engine. v4.1 (Apr 2025) and v4.2 (Feb 2026) are non-breaking additions.
breaking_changes.impact_on_project: Since project is starting fresh with v4, these breaking changes vs v3 are irrelevant. No breaking changes within 4.x line.

deprecations.notes: tailwind.config.js is deprecated in favor of CSS @theme. @apply is discouraged in favor of inline utilities. These are v3->v4 migration concerns, not relevant for new projects.

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: No known CVEs. CSS utility framework has minimal attack surface.

tailwindcss.maintenance_details: Very active. Tailwind Labs (Adam Wathan). v4.0 Jan 2025, v4.1 Apr 2025, v4.2 Feb 2026. Oxide engine battle-tested across thousands of production sites.

vite.notes: @tailwindcss/vite is the primary integration method. Tailwind team moved quickly on Vite 8 support.

shadcn.notes: shadcn/ui fully supports Tailwind v4. HSL colors converted to OKLCH. All components use @theme directive.

reactflow.notes: ReactFlow UI components updated for Tailwind v4 in 12.9.0. Requires specific CSS import order.

roadmap_risks.stability: v4 is now mature after 14 months. v4.1 and v4.2 are additive. No major disruptions expected.
roadmap_risks.ecosystem: Tailwind Plus (premium) expanding but core framework remains open source.

shadcn_ui.versioning_note: shadcn/ui is a component registry, not a versioned package. Components are copied into your project. CLI version is the relevant version indicator.

breaking_changes.notes: CLI v4 deprecates some init flags (--base-color, --src-dir, --no-base-style, --css-variables, create command). These are CLI-only changes, not component-level breaking changes. Components evolve incrementally.

deprecations.notes: Toast component being deprecated in favor of Sonner. CLI flags deprecated: --base-color, --src-dir, --no-base-style, --css-variables. 'create' command deprecated (alias of init).

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: No known CVEs. Components are local code - security depends on underlying primitives (Radix UI, Base UI).

shadcn_ui.maintenance_details: Very active. shadcn (maintainer) + community. Major updates every 1-2 months. Now supports both Radix UI and Base UI as primitive backends.

new_features_march_2026.cli_v4: New --dry-run, --diff, --view flags. Framework templates for Next.js, Vite, TanStack Start, React Router, Astro, Laravel.
new_features_march_2026.skills: shadcn/skills gives AI coding agents context for component and registry workflows.
new_features_march_2026.presets: Design system presets: one-string config for colors, theme, icons, fonts, radius.
new_features_march_2026.base_ui: Base UI support as alternative to Radix UI (Feb 2026).
new_features_march_2026.data_slot: Every primitive now has data-slot attribute for styling.

react_19.notes: Full React 19 support. All components updated.

tailwind_v4.notes: Full Tailwind v4 support. CSS-first config. OKLCH colors.

vite.notes: First-class Vite template in CLI v4.

roadmap_risks.primitives: Dual Radix/Base UI support is new. Stick with Radix for stability.
roadmap_risks.sonner_migration: Plan to use Sonner instead of deprecated Toast component.

breaking_changes.notes: Vite 8.0 (5 days ago!) is a major release. Fully integrates Rolldown (Rust-based bundler) replacing Rollup. @vitejs/plugin-react v6 uses Oxc for React Refresh (drops Babel). This is a BRAND NEW major version - early adoption risk.
breaking_changes.impact_on_project: Since project hasn't started yet, can evaluate Vite 7 (stable, proven) vs Vite 8 (bleeding edge, better perf). Recommendation: start with Vite 7.x unless Vite 8.0.1+ patches land soon.

deprecations.notes: Vite 8 drops Rollup in favor of Rolldown. Plugins using Rollup-specific internals may break. Standard Vite plugins should work.

security_vulnerabilities.recommendation: No known CVEs for Vite 8. Vite 7.x has established security track record.

vite.maintenance_details: Very active. VoidZero (Evan You's company) backing. Vite 8 + Vite+ (commercial) announced. Massive ecosystem.

tauri.notes: Tauri docs verified with Vite 5.4.8. Tauri uses devUrl for dev server and distDir for build output - framework agnostic approach should work with Vite 8. BUT: Vite 8 is 5 days old. No explicit Tauri verification yet. Recommend Vite 7.x for safety or test Vite 8 early.

react.notes: @vitejs/plugin-react v6 for Vite 8 fully supports React 19.

tailwind.notes: Tailwind CSS team moved quickly on Vite 8 support. @tailwindcss/vite updated.

roadmap_risks.rolldown_stability: Rolldown was in beta/RC during Vite 8 development. Now stable in Vite 8.0 but very new. Expect 8.0.x patches.
roadmap_risks.vite_plus: VoidZero announced Vite+ (commercial). Core Vite remains open source. No risk.
roadmap_risks.recommendation: Consider pinning Vite 7.x for launch stability, upgrade to Vite 8.1+ once patch cycle matures (April-May 2026).

tauri_2.10.3 + vite_8.0.0.notes: Vite 8 released 5 days ago. Tauri docs not updated. Likely works but no community verification yet.

tauri_2.10.3 + vite_7.x.notes: Well-established combination. Recommended for project start.

tailwind_4.2.0 + shadcn_cli_v4.notes: shadcn/ui fully supports Tailwind v4 with OKLCH colors and @theme directive.

tailwind_4.2.0 + reactflow_12.10.1.notes: Supported since 12.9.0. Requires specific CSS import order in global.css.

vite_8.0.0 + tailwind_4.2.0.notes: Tailwind team confirmed Vite 8 support quickly.

full_stack_all_latest.notes: Only unverified link is Tauri 2.10.3 + Vite 8.0.0. All other pairs are verified compatible.

stale_decisions.finding: 12.8.2 is 3 minor versions behind latest (12.10.1). Missing React 19 + Tailwind v4 UI component support (added in 12.9.0), plus performance optimizations in 12.10.x.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Update to @xyflow/react 12.10.1. This is required for React 19 + Tailwind v4 compatibility in UI components.

stale_decisions.finding: Vite 8.0.0 released 2026-03-12 (5 days ago). Major rewrite with Rolldown replacing Rollup. Brand new, no patch releases yet. Tauri compatibility unverified.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Pin Vite 7.x for project start. Plan upgrade to Vite 8.1+ after first patch cycle (April-May 2026). Alternatively, test Vite 8.0.0 with Tauri early and report issues.

stale_decisions.finding: React 19.2.4 includes critical security fix for Server Components DoS/Source Exposure. Versions 19.2.1-19.2.3 have INCOMPLETE security patches.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Pin React to >=19.2.4. Even though Tauri doesn't use Server Components, the incomplete patch versions (19.2.1-19.2.3) indicate quality issues in those specific builds.

stale_decisions.finding: shadcn CLI v4 just released March 2026 with significant new features (skills, presets, framework templates). Toast component deprecated in favor of Sonner.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Use CLI v4 for project init. Use Sonner for toasts instead of deprecated Toast component. Stick with Radix UI primitives (not new Base UI option) for stability.

stale_decisions.finding: v4.2.0 is latest (Feb 2026). v4.0 was Jan 2025 - now 14 months mature. CSS-first config is the standard. No issues.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Use v4.2.0. Well-tested, all ecosystem components support it. No action needed.

stale_decisions.finding: 2.10.3 is the latest (Mar 4, 2026). Version range in spec is accurate. Check tauri-plugin-shell is updated for GHSA-c9pr-q8gx-3mgp fix.
stale_decisions.recommendation: Use 2.10.3. Verify all @tauri-apps/plugin-* packages are up to date, especially plugin-shell.

critical_actions: Update @xyflow/react from 12.8.2 to 12.10.1 (required for React 19 + Tailwind v4 UI component support)
Pin Vite to 7.x or explicitly test Vite 8.0.0 with Tauri 2.10.3 before committing
Use Sonner instead of deprecated shadcn Toast component
all_clear: Tauri 2.10.3 is latest and actively maintained, no imminent Tauri 3 disruption
TailwindCSS 4.2.0 is mature and well-supported across all components
shadcn/ui CLI v4 supports Vite template natively
All component pairs except Tauri+Vite8 are verified compatible
No EOL or maintenance concerns for any component
